proven.claim: Prove: when node and ancestor are the same object (same id),
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; Prove: when node and ancestor are the same object (same id),
; the first loop iteration finds the match and returns true.
; Loop: current starts at node → first check: current.id == ancestor.id?
(declare-const node_id Int)
(declare-const ancestor_id Int)
; Transition: first iteration binds current = node
(declare-const iter0_current_id Int)
(assert (= iter0_current_id node_id))
; Precondition: caller passes same node as both arguments
(assert (= ancestor_id node_id))
; Loop body: if (current.id === ancestor.id) return true
(declare-const iter0_match Int)
(assert (= iter0_match (ite (= iter0_current_id ancestor_id) 1 0)))
; Negate: function does NOT return true on first iteration
(assert (= iter0_match 0))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat
; ancestor_id = node_id = iter0_current_id forces iter0_match = 1

proven.claim: Prove: an ancestor two levels above node is found at the third loop iteration.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; Prove: an ancestor two levels above node is found at the third loop iteration.
; Walk: iter0=node, iter1=node.parent, iter2=node.parent.parent=ancestor
(declare-const iter0_id Int)
(declare-const iter1_id Int)
(declare-const iter2_id Int)
(declare-const ancestor_id Int)
; Tree invariant: all IDs in the path are distinct (tree-sitter unique-ID guarantee)
(assert (not (= iter0_id iter1_id)))
(assert (not (= iter1_id iter2_id)))
(assert (not (= iter0_id iter2_id)))
; Ancestor is exactly two levels above node
(assert (= iter2_id ancestor_id))
; Ancestor is NOT at iter0 or iter1 (hasn't been found yet)
(assert (not (= iter0_id ancestor_id)))
(assert (not (= iter1_id ancestor_id)))
; Model the full loop result:
; returns true iff ancestor_id appears anywhere in {iter0, iter1, iter2}
(declare-const result Int)
(assert (= result (ite (or (= iter0_id ancestor_id)
                           (= iter1_id ancestor_id)
                           (= iter2_id ancestor_id)) 1 0)))
; Negate: function returns false despite ancestor being at iter2
(assert (= result 0))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat
; iter2_id = ancestor_id forces the disjunction true, result = 1

proven.claim: Prove: when ancestor_id does not appear on the path from node to root,
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; Prove: when ancestor_id does not appear on the path from node to root,
; the loop exhausts and the function returns false.
(declare-const iter0_id Int)
(declare-const iter1_id Int)
(declare-const iter2_id Int)   ; root — next parent is null, loop exits
(declare-const ancestor_id Int)
; ancestor_id absent from all path steps (structurally different subtree)
(assert (not (= iter0_id ancestor_id)))
(assert (not (= iter1_id ancestor_id)))
(assert (not (= iter2_id ancestor_id)))
; Valid tree structure: consecutive nodes have distinct IDs
(assert (not (= iter0_id iter1_id)))
(assert (not (= iter1_id iter2_id)))
; Loop result: true iff ancestor_id matched any step before null
(declare-const result Int)
(assert (= result (ite (or (= iter0_id ancestor_id)
                           (= iter1_id ancestor_id)
                           (= iter2_id ancestor_id)) 1 0)))
; Negate: function returns true despite ancestor not on path
(assert (= result 1))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat
; No step matches ancestor_id → disjunction false → result = 0, contradiction

proven.claim: Prove: in a well-formed AST, isDescendantOf(logNode, consequence) and
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; Prove: in a well-formed AST, isDescendantOf(logNode, consequence) and
; isDescendantOf(logNode, alternative) cannot both return true.
; consequence and alternative are disjoint sibling subtrees of the same if_statement.
(declare-const logNode_id Int)
(declare-const consequence_id Int)
(declare-const alternative_id Int)
; Well-formedness: consequence and alternative have distinct IDs (separate subtrees)
(assert (not (= consequence_id alternative_id)))
; The path from logNode to root passes through exactly one of {consequence, alternative}.
; Model: the "gating ancestor" on logNode's path is either consequence or alternative, not both.
(declare-const gating_ancestor_id Int)
(assert (or (= gating_ancestor_id consequence_id)
            (= gating_ancestor_id alternative_id)))
; Mutual exclusion: cannot equal both (consequence ≠ alternative by above)
(assert (not (and (= gating_ancestor_id consequence_id)
                  (= gating_ancestor_id alternative_id))))
; isDescendantOf result for consequence: true iff gating_ancestor_id = consequence_id
(declare-const result_cons Int)
(assert (= result_cons (ite (= gating_ancestor_id consequence_id) 1 0)))
; isDescendantOf result for alternative: true iff gating_ancestor_id = alternative_id
(declare-const result_alt Int)
(assert (= result_alt (ite (= gating_ancestor_id alternative_id) 1 0)))
; Negate: both return true simultaneously
(assert (= result_cons 1))
(assert (= result_alt 1))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat
; gating_ancestor_id cannot equal both consequence_id and alternative_id
; when they are distinct — mutual exclusivity is proven

proven.claim: Prove: the while loop terminates for any node in a finite-depth tree.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P7
; Prove: the while loop terminates for any node in a finite-depth tree.
; Each iteration advances current = current.parent, reducing depth by 1.
; Root has depth 0; its parent is null, ending the loop.
(declare-const depth_node Int)    ; depth of node from root (root = 0)
(declare-const max_iterations Int)
; Tree invariant: depth is non-negative
(assert (>= depth_node 0))
; Transition: walk takes exactly depth_node steps to reach root, then 1 more to hit null
(assert (= max_iterations (+ depth_node 1)))
; Negate: walk requires fewer than 1 iteration (impossible for a valid node)
(assert (< max_iterations 1))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat
; max_iterations = depth_node + 1 >= 1 for all depth_node >= 0
; No finite-depth well-formed tree produces a non-terminating walk

violations.claim: Reachable: ID aliasing causes isDescendantOf(logNode, consequence) = true
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; Reachable: ID aliasing causes isDescendantOf(logNode, consequence) = true
; even though logNode is structurally inside alternative.
;
; Scenario: incremental re-parse recycles the freed logNode's old memory address
; for the newly allocated consequence node. Both end up with the same integer id.
(declare-const logNode_id Int)
(declare-const consequence_id Int)
(declare-const alternative_id Int)
(declare-const logNode_parent_id Int)
; Structural fact: logNode is inside alternative, not consequence
; (logNode's direct parent is alternative's subtree)
(assert (= logNode_parent_id alternative_id))
; alternative and consequence are distinct sibling subtrees
(assert (not (= alternative_id consequence_id)))
; Aliasing: consequence_id was recycled and now equals logNode_id
(assert (= consequence_id logNode_id))
; Model isDescendantOf(logNode, consequence):
; iter0: current = logNode, current.id = logNode_id
; Check: logNode_id == consequence_id?  →  logNode_id == logNode_id  →  TRUE (via alias)
(declare-const iter0_id Int)
(assert (= iter0_id logNode_id))
(declare-const result Int)
(assert (= result (ite (= iter0_id consequence_id) 1 0)))
; Violation: function returns true, but logNode's structural parent is alternative_id ≠ consequence_id
(assert (= result 1))
(assert (not (= logNode_parent_id consequence_id)))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat
; Witness: logNode_id = consequence_id (aliased) forces result = 1;
; logNode_parent_id = alternative_id ≠ consequence_id confirms structural non-membership.
; extractPathConditions would then add condition.text (the then-branch condition)
; to pathConditions for a log statement that is actually in the else-branch.

violations.claim: Reachable: if the same if_statement ancestor is visited twice in the outer walk,
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; Reachable: if the same if_statement ancestor is visited twice in the outer walk,
; its condition is added twice — once as "cond" and once as "!(cond)" — a contradiction.
;
; Transition: outer loop processes sibling_A then sibling_B, both wrapping logNode.
; If sibling_A_id = sibling_B_id (aliased or shared node), both visits fire.
(declare-const ifstmt_A_id Int)
(declare-const ifstmt_B_id Int)
(declare-const consequence_A_id Int)
(declare-const consequence_B_id Int)
(declare-const logNode_depth Int)
; Both visits are for the same logical if_statement (aliased or shared)
(assert (= ifstmt_A_id ifstmt_B_id))
; Same if_statement ⇒ same consequence node
(assert (= consequence_A_id consequence_B_id))
; logNode is inside consequence at visit A → result_A = true
; Model: logNode depth is at least 1 below consequence
(assert (>= logNode_depth 1))
(declare-const result_A Int)
(assert (= result_A 1))   ; isDescendantOf(logNode, consequence_A) = true (same if as B)
; At visit B (same if_statement revisited), the else-branch check also fires
; because the outer loop's current.parent advances into an alternative that
; resolves back to the same if_statement
(declare-const result_B_alt Int)
(assert (= result_B_alt 1))  ; isDescendantOf(logNode, alternative_B) also = true (same subtree)
; conditions list now contains both condition.text AND !(condition.text) for same condition
(declare-const conditions_length Int)
(assert (= conditions_length 2))    ; two entries added for the same if_statement
; Violation: path conditions contain a contradictory pair
(assert (> conditions_length 1))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat
; Witness: ifstmt_A_id = ifstmt_B_id, consequence_A_id = consequence_B_id,
; logNode_depth = 1 → both result_A and result_B_alt = 1 → contradictory conditions emitted.
; In practice this requires a non-tree (shared-node) structure, but the function
; has no guard against it and the calling loop has no deduplication.
